page: Microsoft Azure F64-16ams_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. This instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3791.59 and a gzip compression rate of 41.08 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is strong, registering 60126.92 in stress-ng and 246.8 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also robust, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 110.31 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 427.82 tokens/sec using a small 135M LLM model, alongside average performance on larger 7B and 70B models.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core, the F64-16ams_v7 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high core counts or local disk storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for local-disk-heavy databases or GPU-accelerated deep learning. However, its massive memory capacity and strong single-core compute performance make it highly suitable for in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, memory-intensive data processing, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F64-16ams_v7 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the Famsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 16-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features an exceptionally high memory allocation of 512.0 GB, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core, though it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and gzip compression scores in the top 10%, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. The server's memory bandwidth and PassMark memory scores are highly competitive, placing in the top 25%. This hardware profile offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not require extensive local storage. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, memory-intensive data analytics, and lightweight language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64-16ams_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, making it ideal for in-memory databases and data processing.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated high-frequency processors and massive memory density for demanding in-memory workloads and data processing.

512.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier single-core performance and gzip compression speeds
